title: EarthObjects
description: Objects in Earth's orbital neighbourhood (LEO through L2). Single file at data/earth-objects.json. Per-locale editorial in data/i18n/[locale]/earth-objects/[id].json. Field semantics (precision pass): `body` says what the object orbits or is positioned at (Earth, Moon, Sun-Earth-L2). `earth_distance_km` is distance from Earth's surface used by /earth screen for log-scale placement (= altitude_km when body is EARTH). `altitude_km` is altitude above the body's surface (LRO 50 km lunar altitude, ISS 408 km Earth altitude); omitted for L2 objects since Lagrange points are not body surfaces.

body.description: Body the object orbits, or Lagrange point at which it is stationed

earth_distance_km.description: Distance from Earth's surface for /earth log-scale placement; equals altitude_km for body=EARTH; for MOON ≈ 384,400; for SUN_EARTH_L2 ≈ 1,500,000

altitude_km.description: Altitude above the body's surface. Required when body has a meaningful surface (EARTH, MOON); omitted for SUN_EARTH_L2

perigee_altitude_km.description: Periapsis altitude (above body surface) — only for elliptical orbits

apogee_altitude_km.description: Apoapsis altitude (above body surface) — only for elliptical orbits

period_min.description: Orbital period in minutes around the object's body. Omitted for L2 objects (which orbit the Sun, not Earth) and for objects whose Earth-relative motion is best described by their parent body's period (e.g. LRO follows the Moon's 27.32-day period).

inclination.description: Inclination in degrees relative to body equator; omitted for L2

count.description: Number of dots to render (full constellations like GPS show as a sample subset)
